name: CHEMISTRY (Theory)
question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 56/5/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A : Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each.
  3. Section B : Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each.
  4. Section C : Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each.
  5. Section D : Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  7. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

A cube solid is made up of two elements A and B. Element A forms hcp while atoms of element B occupy two-third of the octahedral voids. What is the formula of the solid?

 1
2.

What type of isomerism is shown by complex [Co(NH3)5Cl]SO4?

 1

OR

Arrange the following in increasing order of crystal field splitting energy:
[Cr(CN)6]3-, [Cr(NH3)6]3+, [CrCl6]3-

 1
3.

Oxidation of CH3 - CHO is easier than CH3 - COCH3. Why?

 1
4.

Write the IUPAC name of the following compound:
{tex}CH_3C-\underset{\underset{CH_3}\vert}N-CH_2-CH_2-CH_3{/tex}

 1
5.

How will you prepare arsenic sulphide sol in the lab?

 1

OR

Give an example of heterogeneous catalysis.

 1

SECTION B
6.

What happens when

  1. XeF2 is hydrolysed?
  2. SO2 is passed into aqueous solution of Fe3+ salt?
 2
7.

Complete and balance the following equations:

  1. {tex}\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}{/tex} + I- + H+ {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  2. Na2Cr2O7 + KCl {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
 2
8.

Following reactions may occur at cathode and anode during electrolysis of aqueous sodium chloride. What products will be held at anode and cathode? Use given Eo values to justify your answer.
Cathode: Na+(aq) + e- {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Na(s) Eo = -2.71 V
H+(aq) + e- {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac {1} {2}{/tex}H2(g) Eo = 0.00 V
Anode: Cl-(aq) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac {1} {2}{/tex}Cl2(g) + e- Eo = +1.36 V
2H2O(aq) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} O2(g) + 4H+ + 4e- Eo = +1.23 V

 2
9.

Show that for a first order reaction, time required for completion of 99% of reaction is twice the time required for completion of 90% of reaction.

 2
10.

What happens when D-glucose is treated with the following? Give equations to support your answer.

  1. HI
  2. HNO3
 2

OR

Give any two points of difference between globular and fibrous proteins.

 2
11.

An aromatic compound A on treatment with CHCl3 and KOH gives two compounds, both of which give same product B when distilled with Zinc dust. Oxidation of B gives C with molecular formula C7H6O2. Sodium salt of C on heating with soda lime gives D which may also be obtained by distilling A with Zinc dust. Identify A, B, C and D.

 2

OR

How do you convert the following:

  1. Phenol to Toluene
  2. Ethanol to Ethanal
 2
12.

Define the following terms:

  1. Polysaccharides
  2. Nucleotides
 2

SECTION C
13.

Calculate the emf of the following cell at 25oC:
Al (s) | Al3+ (0.001 M) || (0.1) Ni2+ | Ni (s)
Given: {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\left(\mathrm{Ni}^{2+} / \mathrm{Ni}\right)}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} = -0.25 V
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\left(\mathrm{Al}^{3+} / \mathrm{Al}\right)}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} = -1.66 V
[log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771]

 3
14.
  1. What type of stoichiometric defect is shown by NaCl and why?
  2. Calculate the efficiency of packing in case of a metal crystal for face centred cubic unit cell.
 3
15.

Write three differences between Physisorption and Chemisorption.

 3
16.

The reaction between A and B is first order with respect to A and zero order with respect to B. Fill in the blanks in the following table:

Experiment A/mol L-1 B/mol L-1 Initial rate /mol L-1 min-1
I 0.1 0.1 2.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
II - 0.2 4.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
III 0.4 0.4 -
IV - 0.2 2.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
3
17.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. The only oxidation state shown by Scandium is +3.
  2. [Ti(H2O)6]4+ is colourless.
  3. MnO is basic while Mn2O7 is acidic.
 3

OR

Answer the following:

  1. What is the general electronic configuration of lanthanoids?
  2. What are the common oxidation states of Cerium (At. no. 58)?
  3. Why do actinoids show a wide range of oxidation states?
 3
18.

State the principle involved in the following metallurgical processes:

  1. Froth Floatation
  2. Zone Refining
  3. Vapour Phase Refining
 3

OR

Give one point of difference between the following:

  1. Cast Iron and Pig Iron
  2. Hydraulic Washing and Liquation
  3. Leaching and Roasting
 3
19.

Write the hybridisation and magnetic behaviour of the following complex:

  1. [CoF6]3-
  2. [Ni(CN)4]2-

[Atomic number of Co = 27, Ni = 28]

 3
20.
  1. Define the following terms:
    1. Enantiomers
    2. Racemic mixture
  2. Why is chlorobenzene resistant to nucleophilic substitution reaction?
 3
21.

Account for the following, supporting your answer with diagrams or equations wherever possible:

  1. Diazomium salts of aromatic amines are more stable than those of aliphatic amines.
  2. Methylamine in water reacts with ferric chloride to precipitate hydrated ferric oxide.
 3

OR

Arrange the following in decreasing order of pkb giving reason:

  1. Aniline, p-nitroaniline and p-toluidine
  2. C2H5NH2, (C2H5)2NH, (C2H5)3N in gaseous state
 3
22.

Give one chemical test to distinguish between the following:

  1. Phenol and 1-propanol
  2. Ethanol and dimethyl ether
  3. 1-propanol and 2-Methyl-2-propanol
 3

OR

Write the products of the following reactions:

  1. CH3 - CH2 - O - CH3 + HI {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}
 3
23.

Differentiate between the following:

  1. Antiseptics and Disinfectants
  2. Antacids and Antihistamines
  3. Soaps and Detergents
 3
24.

Answer the following with reason:

  1. Is (NH-CHR-CO)n, a homopolymer or copolymer?
  2. Is PVC a condensation or addition polymer?
  3. Is Bakelite a thermoplastic or a thermosetting plastic?
 3

SECTION D
25.
  1. Give IUPAC name of CH3 - CH = CH - CHO.
  2. How can you distinguish between ethanol and ethanal?
  3. How will you convert the following:
    1. Toluene to benzoic acid
    2. Ethanol to propan-2-ol
    3. Propanal to 2-hydroxypropanoic acid
 5

OR
  1. Give IUPAC name of Salicylic acid.
  2. Chloroacetic acid is more acidic than acetic acid. Why?
  3. Write the products formed when (CH3)3C-CHO reacts with the following:
    1. Zinc amalgam and dilute hydrochloric acid
    2. Concentrated sodium hydroxide solution
    3. Semicarbazide and a weak acid
 5
26.
  1. Draw the graph between vapour pressure and temperature and explain the elevation in boiling point of a solvent in solution.
  2. Determine the osmotic pressure of a solution prepared by dissolving 25 mg of K2SO4 in 2 litres of water at 25oC assuming it to be completely dissociated. (Atomic masses K = 39 u, S = 32 u, O = 16 u)
 5

OR
  1. Write two characteristics of non-ideal solution.
  2. 2 g of benzoic (C6H5COOH) dissolved in 25 g of benzene shows a depression in freezing point equal to 1.62 K. Molal depression constant for benzene is 4.9 K kg mol-1. What is the percentage association of acid if it forms dimer in solution?
 5
27.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. NO2 dimerises readily.
  2. Chlorine acts as a bleaching agent.
  3. In spite of small size, electron gain enthalpy of oxygen is less negative as compared to sulphur.
  4. Unlike chlorine, fluorine forms only one oxoacid, HOF.
  5. Noble gases have very low boiling points.
 5

OR
  1. Draw structure and name the shape of the following:
    1. SF4
    2. ICl3
  2. What happens when (Support your answer with equation)
    1. Chlorine gas is passed through hot and concentrated sodium hydroxide solution?
    2. Xenon hexafluoride is subjected to complete hydrolysis?
    3. Concentrated sulphuric acid is poured over cane sugar?
 5

answer_key_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 56/5/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A : Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each.
  3. Section B : Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each.
  4. Section C : Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each.
  5. Section D : Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  7. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

A cube solid is made up of two elements A and B. Element A forms hcp while atoms of element B occupy two-third of the octahedral voids. What is the formula of the solid?

Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 1
2.

What type of isomerism is shown by complex [Co(NH3)5Cl]SO4?

Solution:

Ionization isomerism

 1

OR

Arrange the following in increasing order of crystal field splitting energy:
[Cr(CN)6]3-, [Cr(NH3)6]3+, [CrCl6]3-

Solution:

[Cr(Cl)6]3- < [Cr(NH3)6]3+ < [Cr(CN)6]3-

 1
3.

Oxidation of CH3 - CHO is easier than CH3 - COCH3. Why?

Solution:

Cleavage of C-C bond in ketones is difficult.

 1
4.

Write the IUPAC name of the following compound:
{tex}CH_3C-\underset{\underset{CH_3}\vert}N-CH_2-CH_2-CH_3{/tex}

Solution:

N,N-Dimethylpropan-1-amine

 1
5.

How will you prepare arsenic sulphide sol in the lab?

Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 1

OR

Give an example of heterogeneous catalysis.

Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 1

SECTION B
6.

What happens when

  1. XeF2 is hydrolysed?
  2. SO2 is passed into aqueous solution of Fe3+ salt?
Solution:
  1. 2XeF2 + 2H2O {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2Xe + 4HF + O2
  2. 2Fe3+ + SO2 + 2H2O {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2Fe2+ + {tex}\mathrm{SO}_4{ }^{2-}{/tex} + 4H+
 2
7.

Complete and balance the following equations:

  1. {tex}\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}{/tex} + I- + H+ {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
  2. Na2Cr2O7 + KCl {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}
Solution:
  1. {tex}2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}{/tex} + 10I- + 16H+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2Mn2+ + 8H2O + 5I2
  2. Na2Cr2O7 + 2KCl {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} K2Cr2O7 + 2NaCl
 2
8.

Following reactions may occur at cathode and anode during electrolysis of aqueous sodium chloride. What products will be held at anode and cathode? Use given Eo values to justify your answer.
Cathode: Na+(aq) + e- {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Na(s) Eo = -2.71 V
H+(aq) + e- {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac {1} {2}{/tex}H2(g) Eo = 0.00 V
Anode: Cl-(aq) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac {1} {2}{/tex}Cl2(g) + e- Eo = +1.36 V
2H2O(aq) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} O2(g) + 4H+ + 4e- Eo = +1.23 V

Solution:

At cathode- H2(g) is produced due to greater Eo value of H+ ion
At anode- Cl2(g) is produced due to over voltage /over potential of oxygen

 2
9.

Show that for a first order reaction, time required for completion of 99% of reaction is twice the time required for completion of 90% of reaction.

Solution:

t = (2.303/k)log[R]o/[R]
Let [R]o = 100
For 99% completion reaction-
t99% = (2.303/k)log (100/1)
k = (2.303 {tex}\times{/tex} 2)/t99%
For 90% completion
t90% = (2.303/k)log (100/10)
Putting the value of k
t90% = (2.303 {tex}\times{/tex} t99% {tex}\times{/tex} log10)/2.303 {tex}\times{/tex} 2
2xt90% = t99%

 2
10.

What happens when D-glucose is treated with the following? Give equations to support your answer.

  1. HI
  2. HNO3
Solution:
  1. n-hexane is formed
    {tex}\underset{CH_2OH}{\overset{CHO}{(\overset\vert{\underset\vert C}H}OH})_4\;\xrightarrow{HI\;,\;\triangle}\;CH_3-CH_2-CH_2-CH_2-CH_2-CH_3{/tex}
  2. Saccharic acid is formed
    {tex}\underset{CH_2OH}{\overset{CHO}{(\overset\vert{\underset\vert C}H}OH})_4\;\xrightarrow{Oxidation}\;\overset{COOH}{\underset{COOH}{(\overset\vert{\underset\vert C}HO}}H)_4\;{/tex}
 2

OR

Give any two points of difference between globular and fibrous proteins.

Solution:
Fibrous  Proteins Globular Proteins
1. Their molecules have long thread like structure. 1. They have folded ball – like structure
2. they have helical or sheet structures 2. They may have three dimensional Shapes.
3. They are in soluble in water but soluble in strong acids and bases. e.g. Keratin, fibroin etc. 3. They are soluble in water, acids and Bases and salts. e.g. Egg albumin, casein insulin.
2
11.

An aromatic compound A on treatment with CHCl3 and KOH gives two compounds, both of which give same product B when distilled with Zinc dust. Oxidation of B gives C with molecular formula C7H6O2. Sodium salt of C on heating with soda lime gives D which may also be obtained by distilling A with Zinc dust. Identify A, B, C and D.

Solution:

2

OR

How do you convert the following:

  1. Phenol to Toluene
  2. Ethanol to Ethanal
Solution:
  2. {tex}CH_3CH_2OH\;\xrightarrow[\Delta]{PCC}\;CH_3CHO\;{/tex}
 2
12.

Define the following terms:

  1. Polysaccharides
  2. Nucleotides
Solution:
  1. Polysaccharides contain a large number of monosaccharide units joined together by glycosidic linkages.
  2. Sugar + Phosphate + base / Nucleoside linked to a phosphate group.
 2

SECTION C
13.

Calculate the emf of the following cell at 25oC:
Al (s) | Al3+ (0.001 M) || (0.1) Ni2+ | Ni (s)
Given: {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\left(\mathrm{Ni}^{2+} / \mathrm{Ni}\right)}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} = -0.25 V
{tex}\mathrm{E}_{\left(\mathrm{Al}^{3+} / \mathrm{Al}\right)}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} = -1.66 V
[log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771]

Solution:

n = 6
Eo = {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\text {cathode }}^0-\mathrm{E}_{\text {anode }}^0{/tex}
= (-0.25) - (-1.66)
= 1.41 V
Ecell = {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell- }}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} (0.059/n) log [Al+3]2 /[Ni+2]3
Ecell = 1.41-(0.059/6)log(10-3)2 /(0.1)3
Ecell = 1.4395 V
Hence, the emf of the following cell at 25oC is 1.4395 V

 3
14.
  1. What type of stoichiometric defect is shown by NaCl and why?
  2. Calculate the efficiency of packing in case of a metal crystal for face centred cubic unit cell.
Solution:

Chapter deleted from the textbook.
Chapter deleted from the textbook.

 3
15.

Write three differences between Physisorption and Chemisorption.

Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3
16.

The reaction between A and B is first order with respect to A and zero order with respect to B. Fill in the blanks in the following table:

Experiment A/mol L-1 B/mol L-1 Initial rate /mol L-1 min-1
I 0.1 0.1 2.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
II - 0.2 4.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
III 0.4 0.4 -
IV - 0.2 2.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
Solution:

The given reaction is of the first order with respect to A and of zero order with respect to B.
Therefore, the rate of the reaction is given by,
{tex}Rate=k{{[A]}^{1}}{{[B]}^{0}}{/tex}
Rate = k [A]
From experiment I, we obtain
{tex}2.0\times {{10}^{-2}}mol\,{{L}^{-1}}{{\min }^{-1}}=k(0.1\,mol\,{{L}^{-1}}){/tex}
{tex}k=0.2{{\min }^{-1}}{/tex}
From experiment II, we obtain
{tex}4.0\times {{10}^{-2}}\,mol\,{{L}^{-1}}\,{{\min }^{-1}}=0.2{{\min }^{-1}}[A] {/tex}, so {tex}[A]=0.2mol\,{{L}^{-1}}{/tex}
From experiment III, we obtain
{tex}Rate=0.2{{\min }^{-1}}\times 0.4mol\,{{L}^{-1}} =0.08\,mol\,{{L}^{-1}}{{\min }^{-1}}{/tex}
From experiment IV, we obtain
{tex}2.0\times {{10}^{-2}}mol\,{{L}^{-1}}{{\min }^{-1}}=0.2\,{{\min }^{-1}}[A] {/tex}, so {tex}[A]=0.1mol\,{{L}^{-1}}{/tex}

 3
17.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. The only oxidation state shown by Scandium is +3.
  2. [Ti(H2O)6]4+ is colourless.
  3. MnO is basic while Mn2O7 is acidic.
Solution:
  1. At + 3 oxidation state, Stable do is obtained
  2. Absence of unpaired electron / no d-d transition occurs
  3. MnO has Mn in +2 Oxidation State
    Mn2O7 has Mn in +7 Oxidation State. Higher the Oxidation State, Higher is the acidic character.
 3

OR

Answer the following:

  1. What is the general electronic configuration of lanthanoids?
  2. What are the common oxidation states of Cerium (At. no. 58)?
  3. Why do actinoids show a wide range of oxidation states?
Solution:
  1. The general electronic configuration of lanthanoids is 4f1-14 5d0-1 6s2
  2. +3 and +4
  3. 5f 6d 7s orbitals are of comparable energies

20.
  1. Define the following terms:
    1. Enantiomers
    2. Racemic mixture
  2. Why is chlorobenzene resistant to nucleophilic substitution reaction?
Solution:
  1. i)The stereoisomers related to each other as non superimposable mirror images are called enantiomers.
    ii)Equimolar mixture of d- and l- form is known as racemic mixture.
  2. In chlorobenzene the lone pair of electrons on halogen atom is delocalized on the benzene ring. These are stabilized by resonance, hence, the energy of activation for displacement of halogen is much greater than alkyl halides.
 3
21.

Account for the following, supporting your answer with diagrams or equations wherever possible:

  1. Diazomium salts of aromatic amines are more stable than those of aliphatic amines.
  2. Methylamine in water reacts with ferric chloride to precipitate hydrated ferric oxide.
Solution:
  1. Due to resonance stabilisation of arene diazonium ion /
  2. Methyl amine being basic, gains a proton from water and releases hydroxyl ions which precipitate hydrated ferric oxide. It can be shown in the form of chemical reaction as:
    CH3 NH2 {tex}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}{/tex} CH3NH3+ + OH-
    FeCl3 {tex}\xrightarrow{3 \mathrm{OH}^{-}}{/tex} Fe(OH)3 {tex}\downarrow{/tex}+ 3Cl-
 3

OR

Arrange the following in decreasing order of pkb giving reason:

  1. Aniline, p-nitroaniline and p-toluidine
  2. C2H5NH2, (C2H5)2NH, (C2H5)3N in gaseous state
Solution:
  1. p-nitroaniline > aniline > p-toluidine
    pKb decreases, basicity increases due to inductive effect.
  2. C2H5NH2 > (C2H5)2NH > (C2H5)3N
    Due to increase in number of ethyl groups, inductive effect increases
 3
22.

Give one chemical test to distinguish between the following:

  1. Phenol and 1-propanol
  2. Ethanol and dimethyl ether
  3. 1-propanol and 2-Methyl-2-propanol
Solution:
  1. Add neutral FeCl3 to both the compounds. Phenol gives violet colouration while, 1 - propanol does not.
  2.  Ethyl alcohol when reacts with iodine and alkali gives yellow precipitate of iodoform which has characteristic odour. Dimethyl ether does not give this test 
  3. Add HCl and ZnCl2 to both the compounds, 2-methyl-2-propanol gives turbidity immediately while 1-propanol does not.
 3

25.
  1. Give IUPAC name of CH3 - CH = CH - CHO.
  2. How can you distinguish between ethanol and ethanal?
  3. How will you convert the following:
    1. Toluene to benzoic acid
    2. Ethanol to propan-2-ol
    3. Propanal to 2-hydroxypropanoic acid
Solution:
  1. IUPAC name of CH3 - CH = CH - CHO But-2-enal.
  2. To both the compounds add Tollen’s reagent, ethanal gives silver mirror while ethanol does not
    2. CH3CH2OH {tex}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{PCC, \ Heat}}{/tex} CH3CHO {tex}\xrightarrow[\text { ii)H}_3O^+]{\mathrm{i)CH}_3\mathrm{MgBr}}{/tex} CH3CH(OH)CH3
    3. CH3-CH2-CHO {tex}\xrightarrow[\mathrm{H}^{+}]{\mathrm{KMnO}_4}{/tex} CH3-CH2-COOH {tex}\xrightarrow[(2) \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})]{\text { (1) } \mathrm{Cl}_2 / \mathrm{P}_4}{/tex} {tex}CH_3-\underset{\underset{OH}\vert}{CH}-COOH{/tex}
 5

OR
  1. Give IUPAC name of Salicylic acid.
  2. Chloroacetic acid is more acidic than acetic acid. Why?
  3. Write the products formed when (CH3)3C-CHO reacts with the following:
    1. Zinc amalgam and dilute hydrochloric acid
    2. Concentrated sodium hydroxide solution
    3. Semicarbazide and a weak acid
Solution:
  1. 2-Hydroxy benzoic acid
  2. In chloroacetic acid Cl- ion has -I effect which decreases the electron density of O-H bond in carboxylic group while in case of acetic acid CH3 group has +I effect which increases the electron density of O-H bond. Therefore chloro acetic acid is more acidic than acetic acid.
    1. (CH3)3C-CH3
    2. (CH3)3C-CH2OH and (CH3)3C-COONa
    3. (CH3)3C-CH=NNHCONH2
 5
26.
  1. Draw the graph between vapour pressure and temperature and explain the elevation in boiling point of a solvent in solution.
  2. Determine the osmotic pressure of a solution prepared by dissolving 25 mg of K2SO4 in 2 litres of water at 25oC assuming it to be completely dissociated. (Atomic masses K = 39 u, S = 32 u, O = 16 u)
Solution:
  1. Boiling point elevation refers to the Increase in the boiling point of a solvent upon the addition of a solute. When a non-volatile solute is added to a solvent, the resulting solution has a higher boiling point than that of the pure solvent
  2. i = 3
    {tex}\pi{/tex} = i CRT
    {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.025 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.0821 {tex}\times{/tex} 298/174 {tex}\times{/tex} 2
    {tex}\pi{/tex} = 5.27 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 atm.
 5

OR
  1. Write two characteristics of non-ideal solution.
  2. 2 g of benzoic (C6H5COOH) dissolved in 25 g of benzene shows a depression in freezing point equal to 1.62 K. Molal depression constant for benzene is 4.9 K kg mol-1. What is the percentage association of acid if it forms dimer in solution?
Solution:
  1. The following are features of non-ideal solutions:
     Raoult's Law does not apply to non-ideal solutions. The interactions between the solute and the solvent (B–B) and the solvent and the solute (A–A) are not the same as the solvent-solute (A–B) interaction, {tex}\Delta{/tex}Hmix{tex}\neq{/tex}0
  2. n = 2 (dimer)
    {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = i Kfm
    i = 1.62 {tex}\times{/tex} 122 {tex}\times{/tex} 25/4.9 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000
    i = 0.504
    {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 2(1 - i)
    {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 2(1 - 0.504)
    = 0.992
    = 99.2%
 5
